items.title: CVE-2026-34926: Actively Exploited Trend Micro Apex One Flaw Added to CISA KEV
items.summary: A critical vulnerability in Trend Micro Apex One security agents is being actively exploited in the wild. CISA has added CVE-2026-34926 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, ordering US federal civilian agencies to apply patches by June 4, 2026.
items.why: Apex One is widely deployed in enterprise environments. Active exploitation with a federal patch deadline means attackers already have working tooling — organizations should treat this as an emergency patch.

items.title: CVE-2026-33032: CVSS 9.8 nginx-ui Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Full Server Takeover via MCP Endpoint
items.summary: A critical unauthenticated vulnerability in nginx-ui is exploitable via its MCP endpoint to achieve complete Nginx server takeover. Scored CVSS 9.8, the flaw has been confirmed actively exploited across 2,600+ exposed instances worldwide.
items.why: nginx-ui is a popular web-based Nginx management panel. An unauthenticated CVSS 9.8 RCE with over 2,600 exposed instances and live exploitation makes this extremely high-priority — any internet-facing nginx-ui installation is at immediate risk.

items.title: APT28 Weaponizes Microsoft Office Zero-Day CVE-2026-21509 Within 24 Hours Against Eastern European Defense Ministries
items.summary: Russia's APT28 (Fancy Bear) exploited Microsoft Office CVE-2026-21509 within 24 hours of its public disclosure in a focused 72-hour spear-phishing blitz targeting nine Eastern European nations. At least 29 emails hit defense ministries (40%), transportation/logistics (35%), and diplomatic entities (25%), delivering cloud-hosted C2 implants.
items.why: APT28's near-zero time-to-exploit of a fresh Microsoft Office CVE targeting NATO-aligned defense entities signals active state-level espionage with exceptional speed. Organizations in these sectors should assume targeted phishing and audit email gateways immediately.

items.title: Iranian APT 'Screening Serpens' Unleashes 6 New RAT Variants Against US, Israel, and UAE Since February 2026
items.summary: Palo Alto Unit 42 is tracking Iranian state-sponsored group Screening Serpens, which dramatically escalated operations after a regional conflict began in February 2026. Between February and April 2026, six new remote access Trojan variants were identified in attacks against targets in the US, Israel, UAE, and two additional Middle Eastern countries.
items.why: Rapidly evolving novel malware tooling from a state-sponsored actor across high-profile geopolitical targets reflects intensifying Iranian cyber operations. Defenders in government, defense, and energy sectors in these regions should immediately review the published IOCs.

items.title: Mandiant M-Trends 2026: Time-to-Exploit Has Gone Negative — 28.3% of CVEs Weaponized Within 24 Hours of Disclosure
items.summary: Mandiant's M-Trends 2026 report, drawn from 500,000+ hours of frontline incident response, reveals exploits are now routinely arriving before or within hours of patches. 28.3% of CVEs were exploited within 24 hours of public disclosure. Threat actors are also shifting focus to backup infrastructure and identity services to maximize ransom leverage, with global median dwell time rising to 14 days.
items.why: This data fundamentally breaks the traditional 30-day patch window paradigm. With nearly a third of CVEs weaponized within a single day of disclosure, organizations must adopt threat-informed prioritization, pre-patch mitigations, and assume-breach postures rather than relying on patch timelines alone.
